Differential harness: decodes every inline corpus fixture and compares\n//! against its golden reference output.\n//!\n//! Passes at 0% agreement — the framework is wired but the decoder is not yet\n//! implemented. Future phases drive the agreement percentage up; the ratchet\n//! assertion below must be tightened as each template is implemented.\n\nuse gribtract_testutil::corpus;\nuse gribtract_testutil::diff::{compare_fixture, CoverageReport};\nuse gribtract_testutil::golden;\n\n/// Minimum acceptable agreement percentage. Raise this as templates are implemented.\nconst AGREEMENT_FLOOR: f64 = 100.0;\n\n#[test]\nfn differential_coverage_report() {\n    let fixtures = corpus::list_fixtures().expect(\"corpus manifest must load\");\n\n    let mut report = CoverageReport::default();\n\n    for entry in &fixtures {\n        // Only run inline fixtures; remote fixtures require a separate fetch step.\n        if entry.storage != \"inline\" {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // Skip DRT=40 (JPEG2000) fixtures when jpeg2000 feature is disabled\n        #[cfg(not(feature = \"jpeg2000\"))]\n        if entry.id.contains(\"drt40\") {\n            eprintln!(\"  [skip-drt40-no-feature] {}\", entry.id);\n            report.fixtures_no_golden += 1; // Count as skipped, not failed\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        report.fixtures_total += 1;\n\n        // Load golden reference — absence is not an error at this phase.\n        let golden_fixture = match golden::load_golden(&entry.id) {\n            Ok(Some(g)) => g,\n            Ok(None) => {\n                eprintln!(\"  [no-golden] {}\", entry.id);\n                report.fixtures_no_golden += 1;\n                continue;\n            }\n            Err(e) => panic!(\"failed to load golden for '{}': {}\", entry.id, e),\n        };\n\n        // Load raw bytes.\n        let bytes = corpus::load(&entry.id)\n            .unwrap_or_else(|e| panic!(\"failed to load fixture '{}': {}\", entry.id, e));\n\n        // Attempt to decode.\n        match gribtract::decode(&bytes) {\n            Err(e) => {\n                eprintln!(\"  [decode-err] {} — {}\", entry.id, e);\n                report.fixtures_decode_error += 1;\n            }\n            Ok(actual_fields) => {\n                let matched =\n                    compare_fixture(&actual_fields, &golden_fixture.fields, &mut report);\n                if matched {\n                    report.fixtures_matched += 1;\n                    eprintln!(\"  [match]      {}\", entry.id);\n                } else {\n                    eprintln!(\"  [mismatch]   {}\", entry.id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    report.print_report();\n\n    assert!(\n        report.agreement_pct() >= AGREEMENT_FLOOR,\n        \"agreement regression: {:.1}% < floor {:.1}%\",\n        report.agreement_pct(),\n        AGREEMENT_FLOOR,\n    );\n}\n","numLines":80,"startLine":1,"totalLines":80}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_b922d3c72c004ba28356d3ff","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! GRIB2 message decoder — section splitter + template dispatch.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Parses raw GRIB2 bytes into [`Field`] values by:\n4\t//!   1. Splitting the byte stream into typed sections (0–8).\n5\t//!   2. Dispatching each section to its template-specific parser.\n6\t//!   3. Assembling a [`Field`] for each complete 3+4+5+6+7 group.\n7\t//!\n8\t//! Only the templates required to decode the current corpus are implemented.\n9\t//! Unsupported templates return [`Error::NotImplemented`].\n10\t\n11\tuse crate::error::{Error, Result};\n12\tuse crate::types::{\n13\t    ComplexExtra, Ensemble, Field, ForecastTime, GaussianLatLonParams, GridDefinition,\n14\t    GridProjection, GridValues, LazyField, LambertConformalParams, Level, PackingInfo,\n15\t    ParameterId, PolarStereographicParams, ReferenceTime,\n16\t};\n17\t\n18\t// ── Byte buffer reader ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n19\t\n20\tstruct Buf<'a> {\n21\t    data: &'a [u8],\n22\t    pos: usize,\n23\t}\n24\t\n25\timpl<'a> Buf<'a> {\n26\t    fn new(data: &'a [u8]) -> Self {\n27\t        Self { data, pos: 0 }\n28\t    }\n29\t\n30\t    fn remaining(&self) -> usize {\n31\t        self.data.len() - self.pos\n32\t    }\n33\t\n34\t    fn need(&self, n: usize) -> Result<()> {\n35\t        if self.remaining() < n {\n36\t            Err(Error::TooShort { needed: n, got: self.remaining() })\n37\t        } else {\n38\t            Ok(())\n39\t        }\n40\t    }\n41\t\n42\t    fn read_u8(&mut self) -> Result<u8> {\n43\t        self.need(1)?;\n44\t        let v = self.data[self.pos];\n45\t        self.pos += 1;\n46\t        Ok(v)\n47\t    }\n48\t\n49\t    fn read_i8(&mut self) -> Result<i8> {\n50\t        Ok(self.read_u8()? as i8)\n51\t    }\n52\t\n53\t    fn read_u16be(&mut self) -> Result<u16> {\n54\t        self.need(2)?;\n55\t        let v = u16::from_be_bytes([self.data[self.pos], self.data[self.pos + 1]]);\n56\t        self.pos += 2;\n57\t        Ok(v)\n58\t    }\n59\t\n60\t    fn read_u32be(&mut self) -> Result<u32> {\n61\t        self.need(4)?;\n62\t        let v = u32::from_be_bytes(\n63\t            self.data[self.pos..self.pos + 4].try_into().unwrap(),\n64\t        );\n65\t        self.pos += 4;\n66\t        Ok(v)\n67\t    }\n68\t\n69\t    fn read_u64be(&mut self) -> Result<u64> {\n70\t        self.need(8)?;\n71\t        let v = u64::from_be_bytes(\n72\t            self.data[self.pos..self.pos + 8].try_into().unwrap(),\n73\t        );\n74\t        self.pos += 8;\n75\t        Ok(v)\n76\t    }\n77\t\n78\t    fn read_f32be(&mut self) -> Result<f32> {\n79\t        self.need(4)?;\n80\t        let v = f32::from_be_bytes(\n81\t            self.data[self.pos..self.pos + 4].try_into().unwrap(),\n82\t        );\n83\t        self.pos += 4;\n84\t        Ok(v)\n85\t    }\n86\t\n87\t    // GRIB2 sign-magnitude 16-bit: bit 15 = sign, bits 14-0 = magnitude.\n88\t    fn read_scale_factor_i16(&mut self) -> Result<i16> {\n89\t        let raw = self.read_u16be()?;\n90\t        let magnitude = (raw & 0x7FFF) as i16;\n91\t        if raw & 0x8000 != 0 { Ok(-magnitude) } else { Ok(magnitude) }\n92\t    }\n93\t\n94\t    // Lat/lon values are signed 32-bit in GRIB2 template 3.0:\n95\t    // bit 31 = sign (0=N/E, 1=S/W), bits 30-0 = magnitude in micro-degrees.\n96\t    fn read_latlon_micro(&mut self) -> Result<i64> {\n97\t        let raw = self.read_u32be()?;\n98\t        let magnitude = (raw & 0x7FFF_FFFF) as i64;\n99\t        if raw & 0x8000_0000 != 0 { Ok(-magnitude) } else { Ok(magnitude) }\n100\t    }\n101\t\n102\t    // Longitude is always 0..360 (unsigned micro-degrees).\n103\t    fn read_longi_micro(&mut self) -> Result<u32> {\n104\t        self.read_u32be()\n105\t    }\n106\t\n107\t    fn skip(&mut self, n: usize) -> Result<()> {\n108\t        self.need(n)?;\n109\t        self.pos += n;\n110\t        Ok(())\n111\t    }\n112\t\n113\t    #[allow(dead_code)]\n114\t    fn rest(&self) -> &'a [u8] {\n115\t        &self.data[self.pos..]\n116\t    }\n117\t}\n118\t\n119\t// ── Extra parameters for DRT=2/3 (complex packing) ───────────────────────────\n120\t\n121\t/// Private alias for the public `ComplexExtra` type.\n122\t///\n123\t/// Using the public type everywhere avoids a conversion step when building\n124\t/// `LazyField`.  The public type is defined in `crate::types`.\n125\ttype ComplexPackingExtra = ComplexExtra;\n126\t\n127\t// ── Intermediate parsing state accumulated across sections ────────────────────\n128\t\n129\t#[derive(Default)]\n130\tstruct FieldBuilder {\n131\t    // From Section 1\n132\t    center: Option<u16>,\n133\t    subcenter: Option<u16>,\n134\t    ref_time: Option<ReferenceTime>,\n135\t    // From Section 4\n136\t    parameter: Option<ParameterId>,\n137\t    forecast: Option<ForecastTime>,\n138\t    level: Option<Level>,\n139\t    ensemble: Option<Option<Ensemble>>,\n140\t    pdt_template: Option<u16>,\n141\t    // From Section 3\n142\t    grid: Option<GridDefinition>,\n143\t    gdt_template: Option<u16>,\n144\t    // From Section 5\n145\t    packing: Option<PackingInfo>,\n146\t    drt_template: Option<u16>,\n147\t    complex_extra: Option<ComplexPackingExtra>,\n148\t    /// Number of packed data values (Section 5 oct 6-9). May be less than\n149\t    /// grid.num_data_points when a bitmap is present.\n150\t    n_packed: Option<usize>,\n151\t    // From Section 6\n152\t    has_bitmap: Option<bool>,\n153\t    /// Actual bitmap bytes (one bit per grid point, MSB-first). Present only\n154\t    /// when Section 6 indicator == 0. Length == ceil(n_grid_points / 8).\n155\t    bitmap: Option<Vec<u8>>,\n156\t    // From Section 7\n157\t    values: Option<GridValues>,\n158\t}\n159\t\n160\timpl FieldBuilder {\n161\t    fn build(self) -> Option<Field> {\n162\t        // All required pieces must be present.\n163\t        let center = self.center?;\n164\t        let subcenter = self.subcenter?;\n165\t        let ref_time = self.ref_time?;\n166\t        let parameter = self.parameter?;\n167\t        let mut forecast = self.forecast?;\n168\t        // Back-fill the reference time into ForecastTime (it's parsed from Section 1).\n169\t        forecast.reference_time = ref_time;\n170\t        let level = self.level?;\n171\t        let ensemble = self.ensemble.unwrap_or(None);\n172\t        let grid = self.grid?;\n173\t        let gdt_template = self.gdt_template?;\n174\t        let pdt_template = self.pdt_template?;\n175\t        let packing = self.packing?;\n176\t        let drt_template = self.drt_template?;\n177\t        let values = self.values?;\n178\t        Some(Field {\n179\t            center,\n180\t            subcenter,\n181\t            parameter,\n182\t            forecast,\n183\t            level,\n184\t            ensemble,\n185\t            grid,\n186\t            values,\n187\t            gdt_template,\n188\t            pdt_template,\n189\t            drt_template,\n190\t            packing,\n191\t        })\n192\t    }\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t// ── Top-level decode entry point ──────────────────────────────────────────────\n196\t\n197\t/// Decode all fields from a concatenated GRIB2 byte buffer.\n198\t///\n199\t/// Handles multiple messages (GRIB2 files may concatenate them). Within each\n200\t/// message, repeated section groups (3–7) produce multiple fields.\n201\tpub fn decode_bytes(bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<Vec<Field>> {\n202\t    let mut fields = Vec::new();\n203\t    let mut pos = 0;\n204\t\n205\t    while pos < bytes.len() {\n206\t        // Peek at next 4 bytes: expect \"GRIB\".\n207\t        if bytes.len() - pos < 4 {\n208\t            break;\n209\t        }\n210\t        if &bytes[pos..pos + 4] != b\"GRIB\" {\n211\t            return Err(Error::BadMagic(bytes[pos..pos + 4].try_into().unwrap()));\n212\t        }\n213\t\n214\t        let msg_len = decode_message(&bytes[pos..], &mut fields)?;\n215\t        pos += msg_len;\n216\t    }\n217\t\n218\t    Ok(fields)\n219\t}\n220\t\n221\t/// Decode one GRIB2 message (starting at byte 0 of `msg`).\n222\t/// Returns the number of bytes consumed (the total message length).\n223\tfn decode_message(msg: &[u8], out: &mut Vec<Field>) -> Result<usize> {\n224\t    let mut buf = Buf::new(msg);\n225\t\n226\t    // ── Section 0 (Indicator, 16 bytes fixed) ────────────────────────────────\n227\t    // oct 1-4: \"GRIB\"\n228\t    let magic = [buf.read_u8()?, buf.read_u8()?, buf.read_u8()?, buf.read_u8()?];\n229\t    if &magic != b\"GRIB\" {\n230\t        return Err(Error::BadMagic(magic));\n231\t    }\n232\t    buf.skip(2)?; // reserved\n233\t    let discipline = buf.read_u8()?;\n234\t    let edition = buf.read_u8()?;\n235\t    if edition != 2 {\n236\t        return Err(Error::UnknownEdition(edition));\n237\t    }\n238\t    let total_len = buf.read_u64be()? as usize;\n239\t    // Section 0 is now consumed (16 bytes).\n240\t\n241\t    // Validate total length against buffer.\n242\t    if msg.len() < total_len {\n243\t        return Err(Error::TooShort { needed: total_len, got: msg.len() });\n244\t    }\n245\t\n246\t    // ── Section iterator (sections 1-7 + \"7777\") ─────────────────────────────\n247\t    let mut builder = FieldBuilder::default();\n248\t\n249\t    // The message body ends just before the \"7777\" end marker.\n250\t    let body_end = total_len - 4;\n251\t\n252\t    while buf.pos < body_end {\n253\t        // Each section starts with: 4-byte length, 1-byte section number.\n254\t        let sec_start = buf.pos;\n255\t        let sec_len = buf.read_u32be()? as usize;\n256\t        let sec_num = buf.read_u8()?;\n257\t\n258\t        if sec_len < 5 {\n259\t            return Err(Error::TooShort { needed: 5, got: sec_len });\n260\t        }\n261\t        // Remaining bytes in this section (after the 5-byte header).\n262\t        let body_start = buf.pos;\n263\t        let body_len = sec_len - 5;\n264\t        let sec_body = &msg[body_start..body_start + body_len];\n265\t\n266\t        match sec_num {\n267\t            1 => {\n268\t                let (center, subcenter, ref_time) = parse_section1(sec_body)?;\n269\t                builder.center = Some(center);\n270\t                builder.subcenter = Some(subcenter);\n271\t                builder.ref_time = Some(ref_time);\n272\t            }\n273\t            2 => { /* Local Use section — skip */ }\n274\t            3 => {\n275\t                let (gdt, grid) = parse_section3(sec_body)?;\n276\t                builder.gdt_template = Some(gdt);\n277\t                builder.grid = Some(grid);\n278\t            }\n279\t            4 => {\n280\t                let (pdt, param, fore, lvl, ens) = parse_section4(sec_body, discipline)?;\n281\t                builder.pdt_template = Some(pdt);\n282\t                builder.parameter = Some(param);\n283\t                builder.forecast = Some(fore);\n284\t                builder.level = Some(lvl);\n285\t                builder.ensemble = Some(ens);\n286\t            }\n287\t            5 => {\n288\t                let (drt, packing, complex_extra, n_packed) = parse_section5(sec_body)?;\n289\t                builder.drt_template = Some(drt);\n290\t                builder.packing = Some(packing);\n291\t                builder.complex_extra = complex_extra;\n292\t                builder.n_packed = Some(n_packed);\n293\t            }\n294\t            6 => {\n295\t                let (has_bitmap, bitmap) = parse_section6(sec_body)?;\n296\t                builder.has_bitmap = Some(has_bitmap);\n297\t                builder.bitmap = bitmap;\n298\t            }\n299\t            7 => {\n300\t                let n_grid = builder.grid.as_ref().map(|g| g.num_data_points as usize).unwrap_or(0);\n301\t                let has_bitmap = builder.has_bitmap.unwrap_or(false);\n302\t                // When a bitmap is present, Section 5 n_packed < n_grid. Use n_packed\n303\t                // for the data unpackers (which only see the \"present\" subset).\n304\t                let n_decode = if has_bitmap {\n305\t                    builder.n_packed.unwrap_or(n_grid)\n306\t                } else {\n307\t                    n_grid\n308\t                };\n309\t                let packing = builder.packing.as_ref().ok_or(Error::NotImplemented)?;\n310\t                let drt = builder.drt_template.unwrap_or(0);\n311\t                let packed_values = decode_section7(\n312\t                    sec_body, packing, drt, builder.complex_extra.as_ref(), n_decode,\n313\t                )?;\n314\t                // Expand packed values into the full grid using the bitmap (if present).\n315\t                let values = if has_bitmap {\n316\t                    expand_bitmap(packed_values, &builder.bitmap, n_grid)\n317\t                } else {\n318\t                    packed_values\n319\t                };\n320\t                builder.values = Some(values);\n321\t\n322\t                // Complete field — flush the builder.\n323\t                let next_builder = FieldBuilder {\n324\t                    center: builder.center,\n325\t                    subcenter: builder.subcenter,\n326\t                    ref_time: builder.ref_time,\n327\t                    ..Default::default()\n328\t                };\n329\t                if let Some(field) = builder.build() {\n330\t                    out.push(field);\n331\t                }\n332\t                builder = next_builder;\n333\t            }\n334\t            _ => { /* Unknown section — skip */ }\n335\t        }\n336\t\n337\t        // Advance buf.pos to the end of this section.\n338\t        buf.pos = sec_start + sec_len;\n339\t    }\n340\t\n341\t    // Consume \"7777\" end marker.\n342\t    let end = &msg[body_end..body_end + 4];\n343\t    if end != b\"7777\" {\n344\t        return Err(Error::TooShort { needed: 4, got: 0 });\n345\t    }\n346\t\n347\t    Ok(total_len)\n348\t}\n349\t\n350\t// ── Section 1: Identification ─────────────────────────────────────────────────\n351\t\n352\tfn parse_section1(body: &[u8]) -> Result<(u16, u16, ReferenceTime)> {\n353\t    let mut b = Buf::new(body);\n354\t    // oct 6-7 of section (indices 0-1 in body): originating center\n355\t    let center = b.read_u16be()?;\n356\t    // oct 8-9: originating subcenter\n357\t    let subcenter = b.read_u16be()?;\n358\t    b.skip(1)?; // master tables version\n359\t    b.skip(1)?; // local tables version\n360\t    let significance = b.read_u8()?;\n361\t    let year = b.read_u16be()?;\n362\t    let month = b.read_u8()?;\n363\t    let day = b.read_u8()?;\n364\t    let hour = b.read_u8()?;\n365\t    let minute = b.read_u8()?;\n366\t    let second = b.read_u8()?;\n367\t    // production status and type of data — not stored in Field currently\n368\t    Ok((\n369\t        center,\n370\t        subcenter,\n371\t        ReferenceTime { year, month, day, hour, minute, second, significance },\n372\t    ))\n373\t}\n374\t\n375\t// ── Section 3: Grid Definition ────────────────────────────────────────────────\n376\t\n377\tfn parse_section3(body: &[u8]) -> Result<(u16, GridDefinition)> {\n378\t    let mut b = Buf::new(body);\n379\t    b.skip(1)?; // source of grid definition (oct 6)\n380\t    let num_data_points = b.read_u32be()?; // oct 7-10\n381\t    b.skip(1)?; // optional list octets count (oct 11)\n382\t    b.skip(1)?; // interpretation of optional list (oct 12)\n383\t    let template = b.read_u16be()?; // oct 13-14\n384\t\n385\t    match template {\n386\t        0 => {\n387\t            let grid = parse_gdt_0(&mut b, num_data_points)?;\n388\t            Ok((0, grid))\n389\t        }\n390\t        20 => {\n391\t            let grid = parse_gdt_20(&mut b, num_data_points)?;\n392\t            Ok((20, grid))\n393\t        }\n394\t        30 => {\n395\t            let grid = parse_gdt_30(&mut b, num_data_points)?;\n396\t            Ok((30, grid))\n397\t        }\n398\t        40 => {\n399\t            let grid = parse_gdt_40(&mut b, num_data_points)?;\n400\t            Ok((40, grid))\n401\t        }\n402\t        _ => Err(Error::NotImplemented),\n403\t    }\n404\t}\n405\t\n406\t/// Grid Definition Template 3.0: Latitude/Longitude.\n407\tfn parse_gdt_0(b: &mut Buf, num_data_points: u32) -> Result<GridDefinition> {\n408\t    let shape_of_earth = b.read_u8()?; // oct 15\n409\t\n410\t    // Scale/value for earth shape (spheroid parameters) — not needed for GDT 3.0 geometry.\n411\t    b.skip(1 + 4)?; // spherical radius scale + value (oct 16-20)\n412\t    b.skip(1 + 4)?; // major axis scale + value (oct 21-25)\n413\t    b.skip(1 + 4)?; // minor axis scale + value (oct 26-30)\n414\t\n415\t    let nx = b.read_u32be()?; // oct 31-34: Ni\n416\t    let ny = b.read_u32be()?; // oct 35-38: Nj\n417\t\n418\t    b.skip(4)?; // basic angle of production domain (oct 39-42)\n419\t    b.skip(4)?; // subdivisions of basic angle (oct 43-46)\n420\t\n421\t    let lat_first = b.read_latlon_micro()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0; // oct 47-50\n422\t    let lon_first = b.read_longi_micro()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0;   // oct 51-54\n423\t\n424\t    let resolution_flags = b.read_u8()?; // oct 55\n425\t\n426\t    let lat_last = b.read_latlon_micro()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0; // oct 56-59\n427\t    let lon_last = b.read_longi_micro()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0;   // oct 60-63\n428\t    let di = b.read_u32be()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0;               // oct 64-67\n429\t    let dj = b.read_u32be()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0;               // oct 68-71\n430\t\n431\t    let scanning_mode = b.read_u8()?; // oct 72\n432\t\n433\t    Ok(GridDefinition {\n434\t        template: 0,\n435\t        num_data_points,\n436\t        nx,\n437\t        ny,\n438\t        lat_first,\n439\t        lon_first,\n440\t        lat_last,\n441\t        lon_last,\n442\t        di,\n443\t        dj,\n444\t        scanning_mode,\n445\t        resolution_flags,\n446\t        shape_of_earth,\n447\t        projection: GridProjection::LatLon,\n448\t    })\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\t/// Grid Definition Template 3.20: Polar Stereographic Projection.\n452\t///\n453\t/// Byte layout (b starts at section octet 15 = first body byte after template number):\n454\t///\n455\t/// | Octets | Field |\n456\t/// |--------|-------|\n457\t/// | 15     | shape of earth |\n458\t/// | 16–20  | earth radius scale + value (skipped) |\n459\t/// | 21–25  | major-axis scale + value (skipped) |\n460\t/// | 26–30  | minor-axis scale + value (skipped) |\n461\t/// | 31–34  | Nx |\n462\t/// | 35–38  | Ny |\n463\t/// | 39–42  | La1 (signed microdegrees) |\n464\t/// | 43–46  | Lo1 (unsigned microdegrees, 0–360) |\n465\t/// | 47     | resolution/component flags |\n466\t/// | 48–51  | LaD (signed microdegrees, latitude where Dx/Dy are specified) |\n467\t/// | 52–55  | LoV (unsigned microdegrees, orientation / central meridian) |\n468\t/// | 56–59  | Dx in metres |\n469\t/// | 60–63  | Dy in metres |\n470\t/// | 64     | projection centre flag (Table 3.5; bit 7=0 North Pole, bit 7=1 South Pole) |\n471\t/// | 65     | scanning mode |\n472\tfn parse_gdt_20(b: &mut Buf, num_data_points: u32) -> Result<GridDefinition> {\n473\t    let shape_of_earth = b.read_u8()?;    // oct 15\n474\t    b.skip(1 + 4)?;                       // oct 16–20: earth radius scale + value\n475\t    b.skip(1 + 4)?;                       // oct 21–25: major axis\n476\t    b.skip(1 + 4)?;                       // oct 26–30: minor axis\n477\t\n478\t    let nx = b.read_u32be()?;             // oct 31–34: Nx\n479\t    let ny = b.read_u32be()?;             // oct 35–38: Ny\n480\t\n481\t    let lat_first = b.read_latlon_micro()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0; // oct 39–42: La1\n482\t    let lon_first = b.read_longi_micro()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0;  // oct 43–46: Lo1\n483\t    let resolution_flags = b.read_u8()?;  // oct 47\n484\t\n485\t    let lad = b.read_latlon_micro()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0;       // oct 48–51: LaD\n486\t    let lov = b.read_longi_micro()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0;        // oct 52–55: LoV\n487\t\n488\t    let dx_m = b.read_u32be()? as f64;   // oct 56–59: Dx in metres\n489\t    let dy_m = b.read_u32be()? as f64;   // oct 60–63: Dy in metres\n490\t\n491\t    let proj_centre = b.read_u8()?;       // oct 64\n492\t    let scanning_mode = b.read_u8()?;     // oct 65\n493\t\n494\t    let projection = GridProjection::PolarStereographic(PolarStereographicParams {\n495\t        lad, lov, dx_m, dy_m, proj_centre,\n496\t    });\n497\t\n498\t    Ok(GridDefinition {\n499\t        template: 20,\n500\t        num_data_points,\n501\t        nx,\n502\t        ny,\n503\t        lat_first,\n504\t        lon_first,\n505\t        lat_last: 0.0,  // not stored in GDT 3.20; derive via projection if needed\n506\t        lon_last: 0.0,\n507\t        di: 0.0,        // increment in metres, stored in PolarStereographicParams\n508\t        dj: 0.0,\n509\t        scanning_mode,\n510\t        resolution_flags,\n511\t        shape_of_earth,\n512\t        projection,\n513\t    })\n514\t}\n515\t\n516\t/// Grid Definition Template 3.30: Lambert Conformal.\n517\t///\n518\t/// Byte layout (b starts at section octet 15 = first body byte after template number):\n519\t///\n520\t/// | Octets | Field |\n521\t/// |--------|-------|\n522\t/// | 15     | shape of earth |\n523\t/// | 16–20  | earth radius scale + value (skipped) |\n524\t/// | 21–25  | major-axis scale + value (skipped) |\n525\t/// | 26–30  | minor-axis scale + value (skipped) |\n526\t/// | 31–34  | Nx |\n527\t/// | 35–38  | Ny |\n528\t/// | 39–42  | La1 (signed microdegrees) |\n529\t/// | 43–46  | Lo1 (unsigned microdegrees, 0–360) |\n530\t/// | 47     | resolution/component flags |\n531\t/// | 48–51  | LaD (signed microdegrees) |\n532\t/// | 52–55  | LoV (unsigned microdegrees) |\n533\t/// | 56–59  | Dx in metres |\n534\t/// | 60–63  | Dy in metres |\n535\t/// | 64     | projection centre flag |\n536\t/// | 65     | scanning mode |\n537\t/// | 66–69  | Latin1 (signed microdegrees) |\n538\t/// | 70–73  | Latin2 (signed microdegrees) |\n539\t/// | 74–77  | lat south pole (signed microdegrees) |\n540\t/// | 78–81  | lon south pole (unsigned microdegrees) |\n541\tfn parse_gdt_30(b: &mut Buf, num_data_points: u32) -> Result<GridDefinition> {\n542\t    let shape_of_earth = b.read_u8()?;    // oct 15\n543\t    b.skip(1 + 4)?;                       // oct 16–20: earth radius scale + value\n544\t    b.skip(1 + 4)?;                       // oct 21–25: major axis\n545\t    b.skip(1 + 4)?;                       // oct 26–30: minor axis\n546\t\n547\t    let nx = b.read_u32be()?;             // oct 31–34: Nx\n548\t    let ny = b.read_u32be()?;             // oct 35–38: Ny\n549\t\n550\t    let lat_first = b.read_latlon_micro()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0; // oct 39–42: La1\n551\t    let lon_first = b.read_longi_micro()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0;  // oct 43–46: Lo1\n552\t    let resolution_flags = b.read_u8()?;  // oct 47\n553\t\n554\t    let lad = b.read_latlon_micro()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0;       // oct 48–51: LaD\n555\t    let lov = b.read_longi_micro()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0;        // oct 52–55: LoV\n556\t\n557\t    let dx_m = b.read_u32be()? as f64;   // oct 56–59: Dx in metres\n558\t    let dy_m = b.read_u32be()? as f64;   // oct 60–63: Dy in metres\n559\t\n560\t    let proj_centre = b.read_u8()?;       // oct 64\n561\t    let scanning_mode = b.read_u8()?;     // oct 65\n562\t\n563\t    let latin1 = b.read_latlon_micro()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0;    // oct 66–69\n564\t    let latin2 = b.read_latlon_micro()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0;    // oct 70–73\n565\t    let lat_south_pole = b.read_latlon_micro()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0; // oct 74–77\n566\t    let lon_south_pole = b.read_longi_micro()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0;  // oct 78–81\n567\t\n568\t    let projection = GridProjection::LambertConformal(LambertConformalParams {\n569\t        lad, lov, dx_m, dy_m, proj_centre, latin1, latin2, lat_south_pole, lon_south_pole,\n570\t    });\n571\t\n572\t    Ok(GridDefinition {\n573\t        template: 30,\n574\t        num_data_points,\n575\t        nx,\n576\t        ny,\n577\t        lat_first,\n578\t        lon_first,\n579\t        lat_last: 0.0, // not stored in GDT 3.30; derive via projection if needed\n580\t        lon_last: 0.0,\n581\t        di: 0.0,       // increment in metres, stored in LambertConformalParams\n582\t        dj: 0.0,\n583\t        scanning_mode,\n584\t        resolution_flags,\n585\t        shape_of_earth,\n586\t        projection,\n587\t    })\n588\t}\n589\t\n590\t/// Grid Definition Template 3.40: Gaussian Latitude/Longitude.\n591\t///\n592\t/// Byte layout (b starts at section octet 15 = first body byte after template number):\n593\t///\n594\t/// | Octets | Field |\n595\t/// |--------|-------|\n596\t/// | 15     | shape of earth |\n597\t/// | 16–20  | earth radius scale + value (skipped) |\n598\t/// | 21–25  | major-axis scale + value (skipped) |\n599\t/// | 26–30  | minor-axis scale + value (skipped) |\n600\t/// | 31–34  | Nx (Ni) |\n601\t/// | 35–38  | Ny (Nj) |\n602\t/// | 39–42  | basic angle (skipped) |\n603\t/// | 43–46  | basic angle subdivisions (skipped) |\n604\t/// | 47–50  | La1 (signed microdegrees) |\n605\t/// | 51–54  | Lo1 (unsigned microdegrees, 0–360) |\n606\t/// | 55     | resolution/component flags |\n607\t/// | 56–59  | La2 (signed microdegrees) |\n608\t/// | 60–63  | Lo2 (unsigned microdegrees, 0–360) |\n609\t/// | 64–67  | Di (unsigned microdegrees/1e6) |\n610\t/// | 68–71  | N (number of parallels between pole and equator) |\n611\t/// | 72     | scanning mode |\n612\t///\n613\t/// GDT 3.40 differs from GDT 3.0 only in octets 68–71: these hold N (a raw\n614\t/// unsigned 32-bit integer, not scaled) instead of Dj.  Dj is left as 0.0.\n615\tfn parse_gdt_40(b: &mut Buf, num_data_points: u32) -> Result<GridDefinition> {\n616\t    let shape_of_earth = b.read_u8()?;    // oct 15\n617\t    b.skip(1 + 4)?;                       // oct 16–20: earth radius scale + value\n618\t    b.skip(1 + 4)?;                       // oct 21–25: major axis\n619\t    b.skip(1 + 4)?;                       // oct 26–30: minor axis\n620\t\n621\t    let nx = b.read_u32be()?;             // oct 31–34\n622\t    let ny = b.read_u32be()?;             // oct 35–38\n623\t\n624\t    b.skip(4)?;                           // oct 39–42: basic angle\n625\t    b.skip(4)?;                           // oct 43–46: subdivisions\n626\t\n627\t    let lat_first = b.read_latlon_micro()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0; // oct 47–50\n628\t    let lon_first = b.read_longi_micro()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0;  // oct 51–54\n629\t    let resolution_flags = b.read_u8()?;  // oct 55\n630\t    let lat_last  = b.read_latlon_micro()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0; // oct 56–59\n631\t    let lon_last  = b.read_longi_micro()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0;  // oct 60–63\n632\t\n633\t    let di        = b.read_u32be()? as f64 / 1_000_000.0;        // oct 64–67\n634\t    let n_parallels = b.read_u32be()?;                            // oct 68–71 (N, not Dj)\n635\t    let scanning_mode = b.read_u8()?;     // oct 72\n636\t\n637\t    let projection = GridProjection::GaussianLatLon(GaussianLatLonParams { n_parallels });\n638\t\n639\t    Ok(GridDefinition {\n640\t        template: 40,\n641\t        num_data_points,\n642\t        nx,\n643\t        ny,\n644\t        lat_first,\n645\t        lon_first,\n646\t        lat_last,\n647\t        lon_last,\n648\t        di,\n649\t        dj: 0.0,   // not stored in GDT 3.40; N replaces Dj\n650\t        scanning_mode,\n651\t        resolution_flags,\n652\t        shape_of_earth,\n653\t        projection,\n654\t    })\n655\t}\n656\t\n657\t// ── Section 4: Product Definition ────────────────────────────────────────────\n658\t\n659\tfn parse_section4(\n660\t    body: &[u8],\n661\t    discipline: u8,\n662\t) -> Result<(u16, ParameterId, ForecastTime, Level, Option<Ensemble>)> {\n663\t    let mut b = Buf::new(body);\n664\t    let _n_coord = b.read_u16be()?; // oct 6-7: number of coordinate values\n665\t    let template = b.read_u16be()?; // oct 8-9: PDT template number\n666\t\n667\t    match template {\n668\t        0 => {\n669\t            let (param, fore, lvl, ens) = parse_pdt_0(&mut b, discipline)?;\n670\t            Ok((0, param, fore, lvl, ens))\n671\t        }\n672\t        1 => {\n673\t            let (param, fore, lvl, ens) = parse_pdt_1(&mut b, discipline)?;\n674\t            Ok((1, param, fore, lvl, ens))\n675\t        }\n676\t        8 => {\n677\t            let (param, fore, lvl, ens) = parse_pdt_8(&mut b, discipline)?;\n678\t            Ok((8, param, fore, lvl, ens))\n679\t        }\n680\t        11 => {\n681\t            let (param, fore, lvl, ens) = parse_pdt_11(&mut b, discipline)?;\n682\t            Ok((11, param, fore, lvl, ens))\n683\t        }\n684\t        _ => Err(Error::NotImplemented),\n685\t    }\n686\t}\n687\t\n688\t/// Parse the common PDT header fields shared by templates 4.0, 4.1, 4.8, 4.11.\n689\t///\n690\t/// Reads octets 10–34 (parameter, time, and level fields).  The caller reads\n691\t/// any template-specific tail octets after calling this function.\n692\tfn parse_pdt_common_header(\n693\t    b: &mut Buf,\n694\t    discipline: u8,\n695\t) -> Result<(ParameterId, ForecastTime, Level)> {\n696\t    let category = b.read_u8()?;  // oct 10\n697\t    let number = b.read_u8()?;    // oct 11\n698\t    b.skip(1)?; // generating process type (oct 12)\n699\t    b.skip(1)?; // background generating process id (oct 13)\n700\t    b.skip(1)?; // analysis/forecast generating process id (oct 14)\n701\t    b.skip(2)?; // hours of cutoff after reference time (oct 15-16)\n702\t    b.skip(1)?; // minutes of cutoff (oct 17)\n703\t    let time_range_unit = b.read_u8()?;    // oct 18\n704\t    let forecast_offset = b.read_u32be()?; // oct 19-22\n705\t\n706\t    let type1 = b.read_u8()?;                     // oct 23\n707\t    let scale_factor1 = b.read_i8()?;             // oct 24\n708\t    let scaled_value1 = b.read_u32be()? as i32;   // oct 25-28\n709\t\n710\t    let raw_type2 = b.read_u8()?;                     // oct 29\n711\t    let raw_scale_factor2 = b.read_i8()?;             // oct 30\n712\t    let raw_scaled_value2 = b.read_u32be()? as i32;   // oct 31-34\n713\t\n714\t    // When second surface type is 255 (missing), zero out its scale/value\n715\t    // to match eccodes behavior.\n716\t    let (type2, scale_factor2, scaled_value2) = if raw_type2 == 255 {\n717\t        (255u8, 0i8, 0i32)\n718\t    } else {\n719\t        (raw_type2, raw_scale_factor2, raw_scaled_value2)\n720\t    };\n721\t\n722\t    let param = ParameterId { discipline, category, number };\n723\t    // Reference time is filled in from Section 1 by the caller.\n724\t    let fore = ForecastTime {\n725\t        reference_time: ReferenceTime {\n726\t            year: 0, month: 0, day: 0, hour: 0, minute: 0, second: 0, significance: 0,\n727\t        },\n728\t        time_range_unit,\n729\t        forecast_offset,\n730\t    };\n731\t    let lvl = Level { type1, scale_factor1, scaled_value1, type2, scale_factor2, scaled_value2 };\n732\t\n733\t    Ok((param, fore, lvl))\n734\t}\n735\t\n736\t/// Product Definition Template 4.0: Analysis or forecast at horizontal level.\n737\tfn parse_pdt_0(\n738\t    b: &mut Buf,\n739\t    discipline: u8,\n740\t) -> Result<(ParameterId, ForecastTime, Level, Option<Ensemble>)> {\n741\t    let (param, fore, lvl) = parse_pdt_common_header(b, discipline)?;\n742\t    Ok((param, fore, lvl, None))\n743\t}\n744\t\n745\t/// Product Definition Template 4.1: Individual ensemble forecast, control and\n746\t/// perturbed, at a horizontal level at a point in time.\n747\t///\n748\t/// WMO GRIB2 Table 4.1:\n749\t/// - Octs 10–34: common header (same as PDT 4.0)\n750\t/// - Oct 35: type of ensemble forecast (Table 4.6)\n751\t/// - Oct 36: perturbation number\n752\t/// - Oct 37: number of forecasts in ensemble\n753\tfn parse_pdt_1(\n754\t    b: &mut Buf,\n755\t    discipline: u8,\n756\t) -> Result<(ParameterId, ForecastTime, Level, Option<Ensemble>)> {\n757\t    let (param, fore, lvl) = parse_pdt_common_header(b, discipline)?;\n758\t\n759\t    let member_type = b.read_u8()?;          // oct 35: Table 4.6\n760\t    let perturbation_number = b.read_u8()?;  // oct 36\n761\t    let _n_ensemble = b.read_u8()?;          // oct 37: number in ensemble (unused by harness)\n762\t\n763\t    let ens = Ensemble { member_type, number: perturbation_number as i16 };\n764\t    Ok((param, fore, lvl, Some(ens)))\n765\t}\n766\t\n767\t/// Product Definition Template 4.8: Average, accumulation, extreme values or\n768\t/// other statistically-processed values at a horizontal level in a continuous\n769\t/// or non-continuous time interval.\n770\t///\n771\t/// WMO GRIB2 Table 4.8:\n772\t/// - Octs 10–34: common header (same as PDT 4.0)\n773\t/// - Oct 35: year (u16), month, day, hour, minute, second of end of interval\n774\t/// - Oct 41: number of time range specifications (n)\n775\t/// - Octs 42 onward: n × 12-byte time range specifications (skipped)\n776\t///\n777\t/// Only the common header fields are surfaced; the statistical window\n778\t/// information is not yet modelled in `ForecastTime`.\n779\tfn parse_pdt_8(\n780\t    b: &mut Buf,\n781\t    discipline: u8,\n782\t) -> Result<(ParameterId, ForecastTime, Level, Option<Ensemble>)> {\n783\t    let (param, fore, lvl) = parse_pdt_common_header(b, discipline)?;\n784\t\n785\t    // End-of-interval timestamp and time-range specs: skip entirely.\n786\t    // Oct 35-40: end time (year u16 + month + day + hour + min + sec = 7 bytes)\n787\t    b.skip(7)?;\n788\t    // Oct 41: number of time range specs\n789\t    let n_ranges = b.read_u8()? as usize;\n790\t    // Each spec is 12 bytes.\n791\t    b.skip(12 * n_ranges)?;\n792\t\n793\t    Ok((param, fore, lvl, None))\n794\t}\n795\t\n796\t/// Product Definition Template 4.11: Individual ensemble member +\n797\t/// time-processed statistical product at a horizontal level.\n798\t///\n799\t/// WMO GRIB2 Table 4.11:\n800\t/// - Octs 10–34: common header (same as PDT 4.0)\n801\t/// - Oct 35: type of ensemble forecast (Table 4.6)\n802\t/// - Oct 36: perturbation number\n803\t/// - Oct 37: number of forecasts in ensemble\n804\t/// - Oct 38-43: end-of-interval timestamp (year u16 + month + day + hour + min + sec)\n805\t/// - Oct 44: number of time range specifications (n)\n806\t/// - Octs 45+: n × 12-byte time range specs (skipped)\n807\tfn parse_pdt_11(\n808\t    b: &mut Buf,\n809\t    discipline: u8,\n810\t) -> Result<(ParameterId, ForecastTime, Level, Option<Ensemble>)> {\n811\t    let (param, fore, lvl) = parse_pdt_common_header(b, discipline)?;\n812\t\n813\t    let member_type = b.read_u8()?;          // oct 35\n814\t    let perturbation_number = b.read_u8()?;  // oct 36\n815\t    let _n_ensemble = b.read_u8()?;          // oct 37\n816\t\n817\t    // Oct 38-43: end-of-interval timestamp (7 bytes: year u16 + month + day + hour + min + sec)\n818\t    b.skip(7)?;\n819\t    // Oct 44: number of time range specs\n820\t    let n_ranges = b.read_u8()? as usize;\n821\t    b.skip(12 * n_ranges)?;\n822\t\n823\t    let ens = Ensemble { member_type, number: perturbation_number as i16 };\n824\t    Ok((param, fore, lvl, Some(ens)))\n825\t}\n826\t\n827\t// ── Section 5: Data Representation ───────────────────────────────────────────\n828\t\n829\tfn parse_section5(body: &[u8]) -> Result<(u16, PackingInfo, Option<ComplexPackingExtra>, usize)> {\n830\t    let mut b = Buf::new(body);\n831\t    let n_values = b.read_u32be()? as usize; // oct 6-9: number of packed values\n832\t    let template = b.read_u16be()?;           // oct 10-11: DRT template number\n833\t\n834\t    match template {\n835\t        0 => {\n836\t            let packing = parse_drt_common(&mut b)?;\n837\t            Ok((0, packing, None, n_values))\n838\t        }\n839\t        2 => {\n840\t            let (packing, extra) = parse_drt_2(&mut b)?;\n841\t            Ok((2, packing, Some(extra), n_values))\n842\t        }\n843\t        3 => {\n844\t            let (packing, extra) = parse_drt_3(&mut b)?;\n845\t            Ok((3, packing, Some(extra), n_values))\n846\t        }\n847\t        40 => {\n848\t            // Template 5.40: JPEG 2000 data compression — same common header as DRT=0.\n849\t            let packing = parse_drt_common(&mut b)?;\n850\t            Ok((40, packing, None, n_values))\n851\t        }\n852\t        41 => {\n853\t            // Template 5.41: PNG data compression — same common header as DRT=0.\n854\t            let packing = parse_drt_common(&mut b)?;\n855\t            Ok((41, packing, None, n_values))\n856\t        }\n857\t        _ => Err(Error::NotImplemented),\n858\t    }\n859\t}\n860\t\n861\t/// Parse the common packing header shared by DRT templates 0, 2, and 3.\n862\tfn parse_drt_common(b: &mut Buf) -> Result<PackingInfo> {\n863\t    let reference_value = b.read_f32be()?;               // oct 12-15: R\n864\t    let binary_scale_factor = b.read_scale_factor_i16()?; // oct 16-17: E\n865\t    let decimal_scale_factor = b.read_scale_factor_i16()?; // oct 18-19: D\n866\t    let bits_per_value = b.read_u8()?;                   // oct 20: N\n867\t    let original_field_type = b.read_u8()?;              // oct 21\n868\t\n869\t    Ok(PackingInfo {\n870\t        reference_value,\n871\t        binary_scale_factor,\n872\t        decimal_scale_factor,\n873\t        bits_per_value,\n874\t        original_field_type,\n875\t    })\n876\t}\n877\t\n878\t/// Data Representation Template 5.2: Complex packing (no spatial differencing).\n879\tfn parse_drt_2(b: &mut Buf) -> Result<(PackingInfo, ComplexPackingExtra)> {\n880\t    let packing = parse_drt_common(b)?;  // oct 12-21: common header\n881\t\n882\t    b.skip(1)?; // oct 22: group splitting method\n883\t    b.skip(1)?; // oct 23: missing value management (0 = none)\n884\t    b.skip(4)?; // oct 24-27: primary missing value\n885\t    b.skip(4)?; // oct 28-31: secondary missing value\n886\t\n887\t    let n_groups = b.read_u32be()?;              // oct 32-35\n888\t    let ref_group_widths = b.read_u8()?;         // oct 36\n889\t    let bits_group_widths = b.read_u8()?;        // oct 37\n890\t    let ref_group_lengths = b.read_u32be()?;     // oct 38-41\n891\t    let length_increment = b.read_u8()?;         // oct 42\n892\t    let true_last_group_length = b.read_u32be()?; // oct 43-46\n893\t    let bits_scaled_group_lengths = b.read_u8()?; // oct 47\n894\t    // Octets 48-49 (order_spatial_diff, extra_octet_count) are absent in template 5.2.\n895\t\n896\t    Ok((packing, ComplexPackingExtra {\n897\t        n_groups,\n898\t        ref_group_widths,\n899\t        bits_group_widths,\n900\t        ref_group_lengths,\n901\t        length_increment,\n902\t        true_last_group_length,\n903\t        bits_scaled_group_lengths,\n904\t        order_spatial_diff: 0,\n905\t        extra_octet_count: 0,\n906\t    }))\n907\t}\n908\t\n909\t/// Data Representation Template 5.3: Complex packing with spatial differencing.\n910\tfn parse_drt_3(b: &mut Buf) -> Result<(PackingInfo, ComplexPackingExtra)> {\n911\t    let packing = parse_drt_common(b)?;  // oct 12-21: common header\n912\t\n913\t    b.skip(1)?; // oct 22: group splitting method\n914\t    b.skip(1)?; // oct 23: missing value management (0 = none)\n915\t    b.skip(4)?; // oct 24-27: primary missing value\n916\t    b.skip(4)?; // oct 28-31: secondary missing value\n917\t\n918\t    let n_groups = b.read_u32be()?;              // oct 32-35\n919\t    let ref_group_widths = b.read_u8()?;         // oct 36\n920\t    let bits_group_widths = b.read_u8()?;        // oct 37\n921\t    let ref_group_lengths = b.read_u32be()?;     // oct 38-41\n922\t    let length_increment = b.read_u8()?;         // oct 42\n923\t    let true_last_group_length = b.read_u32be()?; // oct 43-46\n924\t    let bits_scaled_group_lengths = b.read_u8()?; // oct 47\n925\t    let order_spatial_diff = b.read_u8()?;       // oct 48\n926\t    let extra_octet_count = b.read_u8()?;        // oct 49\n927\t\n928\t    Ok((packing, ComplexPackingExtra {\n929\t        n_groups,\n930\t        ref_group_widths,\n931\t        bits_group_widths,\n932\t        ref_group_lengths,\n933\t        length_increment,\n934\t        true_last_group_length,\n935\t        bits_scaled_group_lengths,\n936\t        order_spatial_diff,\n937\t        extra_octet_count,\n938\t    }))\n939\t}\n940\t\n941\t// ── Section 6: Bit Map ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n942\t\n943\t/// Parse Section 6 (Bit Map).\n944\t///\n945\t/// Returns `(has_bitmap, bitmap_bytes)`:\n946\t/// - `has_bitmap=false, bitmap_bytes=None` when indicator == 255 (all points present).\n947\t/// - `has_bitmap=true, bitmap_bytes=Some(bytes)` when indicator == 0: one bit per grid\n948\t///   point, MSB-first packed, length = ceil(n_grid_points / 8).\n949\tfn parse_section6(body: &[u8]) -> Result<(bool, Option<Vec<u8>>)> {\n950\t    let mut b = Buf::new(body);\n951\t    let indicator = b.read_u8()?; // oct 6\n952\t    match indicator {\n953\t        255 => Ok((false, None)),\n954\t        0 => {\n955\t            // Remaining bytes are the bitmap (1 bit per grid point, MSB first).\n956\t            let bitmap = body[1..].to_vec();\n957\t            Ok((true, Some(bitmap)))\n958\t        }\n959\t        _ => Err(Error::NotImplemented),\n960\t    }\n961\t}\n962\t\n963\t// ── Bitmap expansion ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n964\t\n965\t/// Expand a vector of packed (present-only) values into a full `n_grid`-length\n966\t/// masked grid using the bitmap.\n967\t///\n968\t/// The bitmap is a packed MSB-first bit array (one bit per grid point). Where\n969\t/// the bit is 1, the next value from `packed` is used; where 0, the grid point\n970\t/// is absent (value = 0.0, present = false). Returns `GridValues::Masked` if\n971\t/// a bitmap is provided and `n_packed < n_grid`, otherwise returns the input\n972\t/// as a `Dense` grid.\n973\tfn expand_bitmap(\n974\t    packed: GridValues,\n975\t    bitmap: &Option<Vec<u8>>,\n976\t    n_grid: usize,\n977\t) -> GridValues {\n978\t    let bm = match bitmap {\n979\t        Some(bm) => bm,\n980\t        None => return packed, // no bitmap — pass through\n981\t    };\n982\t\n983\t    let packed_vals = match packed {\n984\t        GridValues::Dense(v) => v,\n985\t        other => return other, // already masked somehow — pass through\n986\t    };\n987\t\n988\t    if packed_vals.len() == n_grid {\n989\t        // The packed count already matches the grid — no expansion needed.\n990\t        return GridValues::Dense(packed_vals);\n991\t    }\n992\t\n993\t    let mut values = vec![0.0f64; n_grid];\n994\t    let mut present = vec![false; n_grid];\n995\t    let mut packed_idx = 0usize;\n996\t\n997\t    for grid_idx in 0..n_grid {\n998\t        let byte = grid_idx / 8;\n999\t        let bit = 7 - (grid_idx % 8); // MSB first\n1000\t        let is_present = byte < bm.len() && (bm[byte] >> bit) & 1 == 1;\n1001\t        if is_present {\n1002\t            if packed_idx < packed_vals.len() {\n1003\t                values[grid_idx] = packed_vals[packed_idx];\n1004\t                packed_idx += 1;\n1005\t            }\n1006\t            present[grid_idx] = true;\n1007\t        }\n1008\t    }\n1009\t\n1010\t    GridValues::Masked { values, present }\n1011\t}\n1012\t\n1013\t// ── Section 7: Data ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n1014\t\n1015\tfn decode_section7(\n1016\t    body: &[u8],\n1017\t    packing: &PackingInfo,\n1018\t    drt: u16,\n1019\t    complex_extra: Option<&ComplexPackingExtra>,\n1020\t    n_points: usize,\n1021\t) -> Result<GridValues> {\n1022\t    if let Some(extra) = complex_extra {\n1023\t        return decode_drt3(body, packing, extra, n_points);\n1024\t    }\n1025\t\n1026\t    if drt == 40 {\n1027\t        #[cfg(feature = \"jpeg2000\")]\n1028\t        return decode_drt40(body, packing, n_points);\n1029\t        #[cfg(not(feature = \"jpeg2000\"))]\n1030\t        return Err(Error::NotImplemented);\n1031\t    }\n1032\t\n1033\t    if drt == 41 {\n1034\t        return decode_drt41(body, packing, n_points);\n1035\t    }\n1036\t\n1037\t    // DRT=0: simple packing.\n1038\t    let r = packing.reference_value as f64;\n1039\t    let e = packing.binary_scale_factor as i32;\n1040\t    let d = packing.decimal_scale_factor as i32;\n1041\t    let two_e = 2f64.powi(e);\n1042\t    let ten_d = 10f64.powi(d);\n1043\t\n1044\t    match packing.bits_per_value {\n1045\t        0 => {\n1046\t            // Constant field: X=0 for all points, so Y = (R + 0) / 10^D = R / 10^D.\n1047\t            let v = r / ten_d;\n1048\t            Ok(GridValues::Dense(vec![v; n_points]))\n1049\t        }\n1050\t        n_bits => {\n1051\t            let packed = unpack_n_bits(body, n_points, n_bits as usize);\n1052\t            // WMO spec: Y × 10^D = R + X × 2^E  →  Y = (R + X × 2^E) / 10^D\n1053\t            let values: Vec<f64> = packed.iter().map(|&x| (r + x as f64 * two_e) / ten_d).collect();\n1054\t            Ok(GridValues::Dense(values))\n1055\t        }\n1056\t    }\n1057\t}\n1058\t\n1059\t/// Decode Template 5.40: JPEG 2000 data compression.\n1060\t///\n1061\t/// Section 7 contains a JPEG 2000 codestream (J2K) or JP2 container. The first\n1062\t/// component carries packed integers X; decoded values follow: Y = (R + X × 2^E) / 10^D.\n1063\t#[cfg(feature = \"jpeg2000\")]\n1064\tfn decode_drt40(body: &[u8], packing: &PackingInfo, n_points: usize) -> Result<GridValues> {\n1065\t    let img = jpeg2k::Image::from_bytes(body)\n1066\t        .map_err(|_| Error::InvalidData(\"JPEG2000 decode failed\"))?;\n1067\t    let comp = img.components().first()\n1068\t        .ok_or(Error::InvalidData(\"J2K: no image components\"))?;\n1069\t\n1070\t    let r = packing.reference_value as f64;\n1071\t    let e = packing.binary_scale_factor as i32;\n1072\t    let d = packing.decimal_scale_factor as i32;\n1073\t    let two_e = 2f64.powi(e);\n1074\t    let ten_d = 10f64.powi(d);\n1075\t\n1076\t    let data = comp.data(); // &[i32] — raw packed integer X values\n1077\t    let values: Vec<f64> = data.iter()\n1078\t        .take(n_points)\n1079\t        .map(|&x| (r + x as f64 * two_e) / ten_d)\n1080\t        .collect();\n1081\t\n1082\t    if values.len() < n_points {\n1083\t        return Err(Error::InvalidData(\"J2K: fewer pixels than grid points\"));\n1084\t    }\n1085\t\n1086\t    Ok(GridValues::Dense(values))\n1087\t}\n1088\t\n1089\t/// Decode Template 5.41: PNG data compression.\n1090\t///\n1091\t/// Section 7 contains a raw PNG image. Pixel values are packed integers X;\n1092\t/// decoded grid values follow the standard formula: Y = (R + X × 2^E) / 10^D.\n1093\t/// Only 8-bit and 16-bit grayscale PNGs are supported (the common GRIB2 case).\n1094\tfn decode_drt41(body: &[u8], packing: &PackingInfo, n_points: usize) -> Result<GridValues> {\n1095\t    let cursor = std::io::Cursor::new(body);\n1096\t    let decoder = png::Decoder::new(cursor);\n1097\t    let mut reader = decoder.read_info().map_err(|_| Error::InvalidData(\"PNG header decode failed\"))?;\n1098\t\n1099\t    let buf_size = reader.output_buffer_size().ok_or(Error::InvalidData(\"PNG: buffer size unknown\"))?;\n1100\t    let mut img_data = vec![0u8; buf_size];\n1101\t    let info = reader.next_frame(&mut img_data).map_err(|_| Error::InvalidData(\"PNG frame decode failed\"))?;\n1102\t\n1103\t    let r = packing.reference_value as f64;\n1104\t    let e = packing.binary_scale_factor as i32;\n1105\t    let d = packing.decimal_scale_factor as i32;\n1106\t    let two_e = 2f64.powi(e);\n1107\t    let ten_d = 10f64.powi(d);\n1108\t\n1109\t    let values: Vec<f64> = match (info.bit_depth, info.color_type) {\n1110\t        (png::BitDepth::Eight, png::ColorType::Grayscale) => {\n1111\t            img_data[..n_points]\n1112\t                .iter()\n1113\t                .map(|&x| (r + x as f64 * two_e) / ten_d)\n1114\t                .collect()\n1115\t        }\n1116\t        (png::BitDepth::Sixteen, png::ColorType::Grayscale) => {\n1117\t            img_data[..n_points * 2]\n1118\t                .chunks_exact(2)\n1119\t                .map(|b| u16::from_be_bytes([b[0], b[1]]) as f64)\n1120\t                .map(|x| (r + x * two_e) / ten_d)\n1121\t                .collect()\n1122\t        }\n1123\t        _ => return Err(Error::NotImplemented),\n1124\t    };\n1125\t\n1126\t    Ok(GridValues::Dense(values))\n1127\t}\n1128\t\n1129\t/// Decode Template 5.3: complex packing with spatial differencing.\n1130\t///\n1131\t/// The Section 7 body contains:\n1132\t///   1. `(order+1) × extra_octet_count` bytes: seed values (ival1, [ival2,] minsd) in\n1133\t///      GRIB2 sign-magnitude big-endian encoding.\n1134\t///   2. `n_groups × bits_per_value` bits (byte-aligned sub-array): group references.\n1135\t///   3. `n_groups × bits_group_widths` bits (byte-aligned): group width offsets.\n1136\t///   4. `n_groups × bits_scaled_group_lengths` bits (byte-aligned): scaled group lengths\n1137\t///      (last group uses `true_last_group_length` from Section 5 instead).\n1138\t///   5. Data values packed within groups (per-group bit width).\n1139\t///\n1140\t/// All sub-arrays are byte-aligned (padded to the next byte boundary between arrays).\n1141\tfn decode_drt3(\n1142\t    body: &[u8],\n1143\t    packing: &PackingInfo,\n1144\t    extra: &ComplexPackingExtra,\n1145\t    n_points: usize,\n1146\t) -> Result<GridValues> {\n1147\t    let order = extra.order_spatial_diff as usize;\n1148\t    let eo = extra.extra_octet_count as usize;\n1149\t    let n_groups = extra.n_groups as usize;\n1150\t    let total_seed_bytes = (order + 1) * eo;\n1151\t\n1152\t    if body.len() < total_seed_bytes {\n1153\t        return Err(Error::TooShort { needed: total_seed_bytes, got: body.len() });\n1154\t    }\n1155\t\n1156\t    // 1. Read seed values from extra octets (sign-magnitude big-endian).\n1157\t    //    For DRT=2 (order=0, eo=0) there are no extra octets — skip reads entirely.\n1158\t    let ival1 = if eo > 0 { read_sign_magnitude_be(&body[..eo]) } else { 0i64 };\n1159\t    let ival2 = if order >= 2 && eo > 0 { read_sign_magnitude_be(&body[eo..2 * eo]) } else { 0i64 };\n1160\t    let minsd = if eo > 0 { read_sign_magnitude_be(&body[order * eo..total_seed_bytes]) } else { 0i64 };\n1161\t\n1162\t    let mut byte_pos = total_seed_bytes;\n1163\t\n1164\t    // 2. Group references: n_groups × bits_per_value bits, byte-aligned.\n1165\t    let nbits = packing.bits_per_value as usize;\n1166\t    let group_refs = unpack_n_bits(&body[byte_pos..], n_groups, nbits);\n1167\t    byte_pos += (n_groups * nbits).div_ceil(8);\n1168\t\n1169\t    // 3. Group widths: n_groups × bits_group_widths bits, byte-aligned.\n1170\t    let bw = extra.bits_group_widths as usize;\n1171\t    let raw_widths = unpack_n_bits(&body[byte_pos..], n_groups, bw);\n1172\t    let group_widths: Vec<usize> = raw_widths.iter()\n1173\t        .map(|&w| extra.ref_group_widths as usize + w as usize)\n1174\t        .collect();\n1175\t    byte_pos += (n_groups * bw).div_ceil(8);\n1176\t\n1177\t    // 4. Group lengths: n_groups × bits_scaled_group_lengths bits, byte-aligned.\n1178\t    //    The last group always uses `true_last_group_length` from Section 5.\n1179\t    let bl = extra.bits_scaled_group_lengths as usize;\n1180\t    let raw_lengths = unpack_n_bits(&body[byte_pos..], n_groups, bl);\n1181\t    let group_lengths: Vec<usize> = raw_lengths.iter().enumerate().map(|(g, &l)| {\n1182\t        if g == n_groups - 1 {\n1183\t            extra.true_last_group_length as usize\n1184\t        } else {\n1185\t            extra.ref_group_lengths as usize + l as usize * extra.length_increment as usize\n1186\t        }\n1187\t    }).collect();\n1188\t    byte_pos += (n_groups * bl).div_ceil(8);\n1189\t\n1190\t    // 5. Packed values within groups (variable bit width per group).\n1191\t    let mut packed = Vec::with_capacity(n_points);\n1192\t    let mut bit_offset = byte_pos * 8;\n1193\t    for g in 0..n_groups {\n1194\t        let w = group_widths[g];\n1195\t        let l = group_lengths[g];\n1196\t        let gref = group_refs[g] as i64;\n1197\t        if w == 0 {\n1198\t            // Zero-width group: all values equal the group reference.\n1199\t            // Extending without any bit reads — common in flat regions.\n1200\t            for _ in 0..l { packed.push(gref); }\n1201\t            continue;\n1202\t        }\n1203\t        // Use specialized extractor for common widths (branch-free), otherwise\n1204\t        // fall back to the generic sliding-window extractor.\n1205\t        let start_bit = bit_offset;\n1206\t        if !extract_group_specialized(body, start_bit, w, l, gref, &mut packed) {\n1207\t            extract_group_windowed(body, start_bit, w, l, gref, &mut packed);\n1208\t        }\n1209\t        bit_offset = start_bit + w * l;\n1210\t    }\n1211\t\n1212\t    if packed.len() != n_points {\n1213\t        return Err(Error::TooShort { needed: n_points, got: packed.len() });\n1214\t    }\n1215\t\n1216\t    // 6+7. Reconstruct integers via spatial differencing and apply the packing\n1217\t    //    formula in a single combined pass (Attempt 5 in parse-speed-log.md).\n1218\t    //\n1219\t    //    Previously: two separate O(n) passes — in-place spatial diff over `packed`\n1220\t    //    (writing i64), then a scaling pass into `values` (reading i64, writing f64).\n1221\t    //    For a 65 K-point field each pass reads/writes ~520 KB; with two passes the\n1222\t    //    vector is read twice from L3/RAM after it exceeds L2.\n1223\t    //\n1224\t    //    Now: one combined pass reads each `packed[i]` exactly once, applies the\n1225\t    //    running-sum state inline, and immediately writes the scaled f64 to `values`.\n1226\t    //    The `packed` Vec is never mutated after construction; `values` is filled in\n1227\t    //    a single sequential write — friendlier for the prefetcher.\n1228\t    //\n1229\t    //    Seed values (ival1, ival2) are emitted directly from the seed variables\n1230\t    //    rather than by overwriting packed[0..order], so no in-place writes happen.\n1231\t    let r = packing.reference_value as f64;\n1232\t    let two_e = 2f64.powi(packing.binary_scale_factor as i32);\n1233\t    let ten_d = 10f64.powi(packing.decimal_scale_factor as i32);\n1234\t    let mut values = Vec::with_capacity(n_points);\n1235\t\n1236\t    if order == 0 {\n1237\t        // DRT=2: no spatial differencing — scale directly.\n1238\t        for &x in &packed {\n1239\t            values.push((r + x as f64 * two_e) / ten_d);\n1240\t        }\n1241\t    } else if order == 1 {\n1242\t        // Position 0 is the seed ival1; packed[1..] hold first-order differences\n1243\t        // (with minsd subtracted by the encoder to keep them non-negative).\n1244\t        if !packed.is_empty() {\n1245\t            values.push((r + ival1 as f64 * two_e) / ten_d);\n1246\t        }\n1247\t        let mut prev = ival1;\n1248\t        for i in 1..packed.len() {\n1249\t            prev += packed[i] + minsd;\n1250\t            values.push((r + prev as f64 * two_e) / ten_d);\n1251\t        }\n1252\t    } else if order == 2 {\n1253\t        // Positions 0 and 1 are seeds ival1/ival2; packed[2..] hold second-order\n1254\t        // differences. The first-order difference at position 1 is ival2 − ival1.\n1255\t        if !packed.is_empty() {\n1256\t            values.push((r + ival1 as f64 * two_e) / ten_d);\n1257\t        }\n1258\t        if packed.len() >= 2 {\n1259\t            values.push((r + ival2 as f64 * two_e) / ten_d);\n1260\t        }\n1261\t        let mut delta = ival2 - ival1; // first-order diff at position 1\n1262\t        let mut prev = ival2;\n1263\t        for i in 2..packed.len() {\n1264\t            let second_diff = packed[i] + minsd;\n1265\t            delta += second_diff;\n1266\t            prev += delta;\n1267\t            values.push((r + prev as f64 * two_e) / ten_d);\n1268\t        }\n1269\t    }\n1270\t\n1271\t    Ok(GridValues::Dense(values))\n1272\t}\n1273\t\n1274\t/// Read a GRIB2 sign-magnitude big-endian integer from `bytes`.\n1275\t///\n1276\t/// The MSB of the first byte is the sign (1 = negative); the remaining bits\n1277\t/// form the unsigned magnitude.\n1278\tfn read_sign_magnitude_be(bytes: &[u8]) -> i64 {\n1279\t    let n = bytes.len();\n1280\t    debug_assert!(n > 0 && n <= 7, \"sign-magnitude: unsupported width {n}\");\n1281\t    let mut raw = 0u64;\n1282\t    for &b in bytes {\n1283\t        raw = (raw << 8) | b as u64;\n1284\t    }\n1285\t    let sign_bit = 1u64 << (n * 8 - 1);\n1286\t    let magnitude = (raw & (sign_bit - 1)) as i64;\n1287\t    if raw & sign_bit != 0 { -magnitude } else { magnitude }\n1288\t}\n1289\t\n1290\t/// Extract `count` values each `w` bits wide from `data` starting at `start_bit`,\n1291\t/// pushing `gref + raw_value` onto `out` for each.\n1292\t///\n1293\t/// Uses a left-aligned u64 sliding window (MSB-first, matching GRIB2 bit order)\n1294\t/// instead of per-element [`read_bits_at`] calls.  The key savings vs. the\n1295\t/// per-element path:\n1296\t///\n1297\t/// * No `div_ceil` per element — `bytes_needed` is computed once on pre-fill.\n1298\t/// * No inner byte-assembly loop per element — bytes are amortised over a\n1299\t///   window: one byte loaded per 8 bits consumed on average.\n1300\t/// * Single `shift + mask` per value instead of 3–9 byte-load + shift operations.\n1301\t///\n1302\t/// The left-aligned invariant: valid bits always occupy the MSB positions of\n1303\t/// `buf`, so the next `w`-bit value is always extracted as `(buf >> (64 - w)) & mask`.\n1304\t///\n1305\t/// # Panics (debug only)\n1306\t/// `w` must satisfy `0 < w ≤ 32`.  The caller is responsible for the w=0 fast\n1307\t/// path (zero-width groups).\n1308\t#[inline]\n1309\tfn extract_group_windowed(\n1310\t    data: &[u8],\n1311\t    start_bit: usize,\n1312\t    w: usize,\n1313\t    count: usize,\n1314\t    gref: i64,\n1315\t    out: &mut Vec<i64>,\n1316\t) {\n1317\t    debug_assert!(w > 0 && w <= 32, \"w={w} out of range (caller must handle w=0)\");\n1318\t    let mask: u64 = (1u64 << w) - 1;\n1319\t\n1320\t    // `buf` holds up to 64 bits of stream data, left-aligned (MSB = next bit).\n1321\t    // `buf_bits` tracks how many valid bits sit at the top of `buf`.\n1322\t    let mut buf: u64 = 0;\n1323\t    let mut buf_bits: usize = 0;\n1324\t    let mut byte_pos = start_bit / 8;\n1325\t    let skip = start_bit % 8; // alignment bits to discard before the first value\n1326\t\n1327\t    // Inline loader: place data[byte_pos] at the next available MSB position.\n1328\t    // Precondition: buf_bits ≤ 56 (so buf_bits + 8 ≤ 64, no overflow).\n1329\t    // buf_bits stays ≤ 40 in the inner loop (analysis below), so this always holds.\n1330\t    macro_rules! load_byte {\n1331\t        () => {\n1332\t            if byte_pos < data.len() {\n1333\t                buf |= (data[byte_pos] as u64) << (56 - buf_bits);\n1334\t                buf_bits += 8;\n1335\t            }\n1336\t            // Always advance byte_pos (prevents infinite loop on malformed data).\n1337\t            byte_pos += 1;\n1338\t        };\n1339\t    }\n1340\t\n1341\t    // Pre-fill: load enough bytes so buf holds at least (skip + w) valid bits.\n1342\t    // Worst case: skip=7, w=32 → 39 bits → 5 bytes → buf_bits ≤ 40 after pre-fill.\n1343\t    let init_bytes = (skip + w + 7) / 8;\n1344\t    for _ in 0..init_bytes {\n1345\t        load_byte!();\n1346\t    }\n1347\t\n1348\t    // Discard the alignment prefix (bits before start_bit within the first byte).\n1349\t    if skip > 0 {\n1350\t        buf <<= skip;\n1351\t        buf_bits -= skip;\n1352\t    }\n1353\t\n1354\t    // Main extraction loop.\n1355\t    // After `buf <<= w`, buf_bits decreases by w.  After at most ceil(w/8)\n1356\t    // refills (each adding 8 bits), buf_bits ≤ w + 8*ceil(w/8) ≤ 40 for w ≤ 32.\n1357\t    for _ in 0..count {\n1358\t        // Refill if needed (at most ceil(w/8) ≤ 4 iterations for w ≤ 32).\n1359\t        while buf_bits < w {\n1360\t            load_byte!();\n1361\t        }\n1362\t\n1363\t        // Extract the top w bits and advance the window.\n1364\t        let v = (buf >> (64 - w)) & mask;\n1365\t        buf <<= w;\n1366\t        buf_bits -= w;\n1367\t\n1368\t        out.push(gref + v as i64);\n1369\t    }\n1370\t}\n1371\t\n1372\t/// Specialized extractor for w=4 (4-bit values, branch-free).\n1373\t///\n1374\t/// Each value consumes exactly 4 bits. Two values = 1 byte.\n1375\t/// This specialization eliminates the while loop by loading 1 byte every 2 iterations.\n1376\t#[inline]\n1377\tfn extract_group_w4(\n1378\t    data: &[u8],\n1379\t    start_bit: usize,\n1380\t    count: usize,\n1381\t    gref: i64,\n1382\t    out: &mut Vec<i64>,\n1383\t) {\n1384\t    let mut byte_pos = start_bit / 8;\n1385\t    let skip = start_bit % 8;\n1386\t\n1387\t    // Load first byte\n1388\t    let mut buf: u64 = if byte_pos < data.len() { data[byte_pos] as u64 } else { 0 };\n1389\t    byte_pos += 1;\n1390\t    let mut buf_bits = 8usize;\n1391\t\n1392\t    // Discard skip bits\n1393\t    if skip > 0 {\n1394\t        buf <<= skip;\n1395\t        buf_bits -= skip;\n1396\t    }\n1397\t\n1398\t    for i in 0..count {\n1399\t        // Refill if needed (every 2 iterations we need a new byte for w=4)\n1400\t        if buf_bits < 4 {\n1401\t            if byte_pos < data.len() {\n1402\t                buf = (buf << 8) | (data[byte_pos] as u64);\n1403\t                buf_bits += 8;\n1404\t            }\n1405\t            byte_pos += 1;\n1406\t        }\n1407\t\n1408\t        // Extract 4 bits\n1409\t        let v = (buf >> 60) & 0xF;\n1410\t        buf <<= 4;\n1411\t        buf_bits -= 4;\n1412\t        out.push(gref + v as i64);\n1413\t    }\n1414\t}\n1415\t\n1416\t/// Specialized extractor for w=8 (8-bit values, branch-free).\n1417\t///\n1418\t/// Each value consumes exactly 1 byte.\n1419\t#[inline]\n1420\tfn extract_group_w8(\n1421\t    data: &[u8],\n1422\t    start_bit: usize,\n1423\t    count: usize,\n1424\t    gref: i64,\n1425\t    out: &mut Vec<i64>,\n1426\t) {\n1427\t    let mut byte_pos = start_bit / 8 + (start_bit % 8 + 7) / 8;\n1428\t    let skip = start_bit % 8;\n1429\t\n1430\t    // Handle non-byte-aligned start\n1431\t    let mut pending: u64 = 0;\n1432\t    let mut pending_bits = 0usize;\n1433\t\n1434\t    if skip > 0 {\n1435\t        if byte_pos > 0 && byte_pos <= data.len() {\n1436\t            pending = data[byte_pos - 1] as u64;\n1437\t            pending_bits = 8 - skip;\n1438\t        }\n1439\t    }\n1440\t\n1441\t    for i in 0..count {\n1442\t        if pending_bits >= 8 {\n1443\t            let v = (pending >> (pending_bits - 8)) & 0xFF;\n1444\t            pending_bits -= 8;\n1445\t            out.push(gref + v as i64);\n1446\t        } else {\n1447\t            if byte_pos < data.len() {\n1448\t                let byte = data[byte_pos] as u64;\n1449\t                if pending_bits > 0 {\n1450\t                    let v = ((pending << (8 - pending_bits)) | (byte >> pending_bits)) & 0xFF;\n1451\t                    pending = byte & ((1u64 << pending_bits) - 1);\n1452\t                    pending_bits = 0;\n1453\t                    out.push(gref + v as i64);\n1454\t                } else {\n1455\t                    out.push(gref + byte as i64);\n1456\t                }\n1457\t            } else if pending_bits >= 8 {\n1458\t                let v = (pending >> (pending_bits - 8)) & 0xFF;\n1459\t                pending_bits -= 8;\n1460\t                out.push(gref + v as i64);\n1461\t            } else {\n1462\t                out.push(gref);\n1463\t            }\n1464\t            byte_pos += 1;\n1465\t        }\n1466\t    }\n1467\t}\n1468\t\n1469\t/// Specialized extractor for w=12 (12-bit values, branch-free).\n1470\t///\n1471\t/// Each value consumes 12 bits = 1.5 bytes. Two values = 3 bytes.\n1472\t#[inline]\n1473\tfn extract_group_w12(\n1474\t    data: &[u8],\n1475\t    start_bit: usize,\n1476\t    count: usize,\n1477\t    gref: i64,\n1478\t    out: &mut Vec<i64>,\n1479\t) {\n1480\t    let mut byte_pos = start_bit / 8;\n1481\t    let skip = start_bit % 8;\n1482\t\n1483\t    // Load initial bytes (need at least skip + 12 bits = skip + 2 bytes worst case)\n1484\t    let mut buf: u64 = 0;\n1485\t    let mut buf_bits = 0usize;\n1486\t\n1487\t    // Pre-fill\n1488\t    let init_bytes = (skip + 12 + 7) / 8;\n1489\t    for _ in 0..init_bytes {\n1490\t        if byte_pos < data.len() {\n1491\t            buf = (buf << 8) | (data[byte_pos] as u64);\n1492\t            buf_bits += 8;\n1493\t        }\n1494\t        byte_pos += 1;\n1495\t    }\n1496\t\n1497\t    // Discard skip bits\n1498\t    buf <<= skip;\n1499\t    buf_bits -= skip;\n1500\t\n1501\t    for _ in 0..count {\n1502\t        // For w=12, we need 2 bytes every iteration (worst case)\n1503\t        while buf_bits < 12 {\n1504\t            if byte_pos < data.len() {\n1505\t                buf = (buf << 8) | (data[byte_pos] as u64);\n1506\t                buf_bits += 8;\n1507\t            }\n1508\t            byte_pos += 1;\n1509\t        }\n1510\t\n1511\t        let v = (buf >> 52) & 0xFFF;\n1512\t        buf <<= 12;\n1513\t        buf_bits -= 12;\n1514\t        out.push(gref + v as i64);\n1515\t    }\n1516\t}\n1517\t\n1518\t/// Specialized extractor for w=16 (16-bit values, branch-free).\n1519\t///\n1520\t/// Each value consumes exactly 2 bytes.\n1521\t#[inline]\n1522\tfn extract_group_w16(\n1523\t    data: &[u8],\n1524\t    start_bit: usize,\n1525\t    count: usize,\n1526\t    gref: i64,\n1527\t    out: &mut Vec<i64>,\n1528\t) {\n1529\t    let mut byte_pos = start_bit / 8;\n1530\t    let skip = start_bit % 8;\n1531\t\n1532\t    // Load initial bytes\n1533\t    let mut buf: u64 = 0;\n1534\t    let mut buf_bits = 0usize;\n1535\t\n1536\t    let init_bytes = (skip + 16 + 7) / 8;\n1537\t    for _ in 0..init_bytes {\n1538\t        if byte_pos < data.len() {\n1539\t            buf = (buf << 8) | (data[byte_pos] as u64);\n1540\t            buf_bits += 8;\n1541\t        }\n1542\t        byte_pos += 1;\n1543\t    }\n1544\t\n1545\t    buf <<= skip;\n1546\t    buf_bits -= skip;\n1547\t\n1548\t    for _ in 0..count {\n1549\t        // For w=16, we need 2 bytes\n1550\t        while buf_bits < 16 {\n1551\t            if byte_pos < data.len() {\n1552\t                buf = (buf << 8) | (data[byte_pos] as u64);\n1553\t                buf_bits += 8;\n1554\t            }\n1555\t            byte_pos += 1;\n1556\t        }\n1557\t\n1558\t        let v = (buf >> 48) & 0xFFFF;\n1559\t        buf <<= 16;\n1560\t        buf_bits -= 16;\n1561\t        out.push(gref + v as i64);\n1562\t    }\n1563\t}\n1564\t\n1565\t/// Dispatch to specialized extractor based on width.\n1566\t///\n1567\t/// Returns true if a specialized extractor was used, false if the generic\n1568\t/// windowed extractor was used instead.\n1569\t#[inline]\n1570\tfn extract_group_specialized(\n1571\t    data: &[u8],\n1572\t    start_bit: usize,\n1573\t    w: usize,\n1574\t    count: usize,\n1575\t    gref: i64,\n1576\t    out: &mut Vec<i64>,\n1577\t) -> bool {\n1578\t    match w {\n1579\t        4 => {\n1580\t            extract_group_w4(data, start_bit, count, gref, out);\n1581\t            true\n1582\t        }\n1583\t        8 => {\n1584\t            extract_group_w8(data, start_bit, count, gref, out);\n1585\t            true\n1586\t        }\n1587\t        12 => {\n1588\t            extract_group_w12(data, start_bit, count, gref, out);\n1589\t            true\n1590\t        }\n1591\t        16 => {\n1592\t            extract_group_w16(data, start_bit, count, gref, out);\n1593\t            true\n1594\t        }\n1595\t        _ => false, // Use generic path\n1596\t    }\n1597\t}\n1598\t\n1599\t/// Read `n_bits` bits from `data` starting at `bit_offset` (MSB-first).\n1600\tfn read_bits_at(data: &[u8], bit_offset: usize, n_bits: usize) -> u64 {\n1601\t    if n_bits == 0 {\n1602\t        return 0;\n1603\t    }\n1604\t    let byte_start = bit_offset / 8;\n1605\t    let bit_start = bit_offset % 8;\n1606\t    let bits_needed = bit_start + n_bits;\n1607\t    let bytes_needed = bits_needed.div_ceil(8);\n1608\t    let mut raw: u64 = 0;\n1609\t    for i in 0..bytes_needed {\n1610\t        let byte = if byte_start + i < data.len() { data[byte_start + i] } else { 0 };\n1611\t        raw = (raw << 8) | byte as u64;\n1612\t    }\n1613\t    let total_bits = bytes_needed * 8;\n1614\t    let shift = total_bits - bit_start - n_bits;\n1615\t    let mask = (1u64 << n_bits) - 1;\n1616\t    (raw >> shift) & mask\n1617\t}\n1618\t\n1619\t/// Extract `count` unsigned integers each `n_bits` wide from `data`.\n1620\t/// Bits are packed MSB-first: the first value occupies bits [0..n_bits-1]\n1621\t/// of the bitstream (bit 0 = MSB of data[0]).\n1622\tfn unpack_n_bits(data: &[u8], count: usize, n_bits: usize) -> Vec<u64> {\n1623\t    if n_bits == 0 {\n1624\t        return vec![0u64; count];\n1625\t    }\n1626\t    // Fast paths for byte-aligned widths — avoids per-element shift/mask\n1627\t    // overhead and exposes the inner loops to compiler auto-vectorization.\n1628\t    match n_bits {\n1629\t        8 => {\n1630\t            let available = count.min(data.len());\n1631\t            let mut v: Vec<u64> = data[..available].iter().map(|&b| b as u64).collect();\n1632\t            v.resize(count, 0);\n1633\t            return v;\n1634\t        }\n1635\t        16 => {\n1636\t            let available = count.min(data.len() / 2);\n1637\t            let mut v: Vec<u64> = data.chunks_exact(2)\n1638\t                .take(available)\n1639\t                .map(|c| u16::from_be_bytes([c[0], c[1]]) as u64)\n1640\t                .collect();\n1641\t            v.resize(count, 0);\n1642\t            return v;\n1643\t        }\n1644\t        32 => {\n1645\t            let available = count.min(data.len() / 4);\n1646\t            let mut v: Vec<u64> = data.chunks_exact(4)\n1647\t                .take(available)\n1648\t                .map(|c| u32::from_be_bytes([c[0], c[1], c[2], c[3]]) as u64)\n1649\t                .collect();\n1650\t            v.resize(count, 0);\n1651\t            return v;\n1652\t        }\n1653\t        _ => {}\n1654\t    }\n1655\t\n1656\t    let mut values = Vec::with_capacity(count);\n1657\t    let mut bit_offset = 0usize;\n1658\t\n1659\t    for _ in 0..count {\n1660\t        let byte_start = bit_offset / 8;\n1661\t        let bit_start = bit_offset % 8;\n1662\t        let bits_needed = bit_start + n_bits;\n1663\t        let bytes_needed = bits_needed.div_ceil(8);\n1664\t\n1665\t        let mut raw: u64 = 0;\n1666\t        for i in 0..bytes_needed {\n1667\t            let byte = if byte_start + i < data.len() { data[byte_start + i] } else { 0 };\n1668\t            raw = (raw << 8) | byte as u64;\n1669\t        }\n1670\t\n1671\t        // raw has bytes_needed * 8 bits, value lives at the top n_bits\n1672\t        // after shifting past the leading bit_start bits.\n1673\t        let total_bits = bytes_needed * 8;\n1674\t        let shift = total_bits - bit_start - n_bits;\n1675\t        let mask = (1u64 << n_bits) - 1;\n1676\t        values.push((raw >> shift) & mask);\n1677\t\n1678\t        bit_offset += n_bits;\n1679\t    }\n1680\t\n1681\t    values\n1682\t}\n1683\t\n1684\t// ── Lazy / partial decode (DRT=0 only) ───────────────────────────────────────\n1685\t\n1686\t/// Decode a single grid point from a DRT=0 (simple packing) Section 7 body.\n1687\t///\n1688\t/// Returns `None` when `idx` is out of range for the given body length and\n1689\t/// packing, or when `bits_per_value` is non-zero but the bit span for `idx`\n1690\t/// exceeds the body.  Returns a value for all indices when `bits_per_value == 0`\n1691\t/// (constant field).\n1692\tpub fn decode_point_drt0(body: &[u8], packing: &PackingInfo, idx: usize) -> Option<f64> {\n1693\t    let r = packing.reference_value as f64;\n1694\t    let two_e = 2f64.powi(packing.binary_scale_factor as i32);\n1695\t    let ten_d = 10f64.powi(packing.decimal_scale_factor as i32);\n1696\t\n1697\t    if packing.bits_per_value == 0 {\n1698\t        return Some(r / ten_d);\n1699\t    }\n1700\t\n1701\t    let n = packing.bits_per_value as usize;\n1702\t    let bit_offset = idx * n;\n1703\t    let byte_end = (bit_offset + n).div_ceil(8);\n1704\t    if byte_end > body.len() {\n1705\t        return None;\n1706\t    }\n1707\t    let x = read_bits_at(body, bit_offset, n);\n1708\t    Some((r + x as f64 * two_e) / ten_d)\n1709\t}\n1710\t\n1711\t/// Decode all grid values from a DRT=2 or DRT=3 (complex packing) Section 7 body.\n1712\t///\n1713\t/// This is the public entry point for the \"decode-once-extract-many\" pattern:\n1714\t/// decode the full grid once and cache the `Vec<f64>`, then do O(1) index\n1715\t/// lookups for each station.  For DRT=3 (spatial differencing), random access\n1716\t/// is impossible without a full decode because each value depends on all prior\n1717\t/// values in the differencing reversal step.\n1718\t///\n1719\t/// `body` is the raw Section 7 body (from `LazyField::section7_raw`).\n1720\t/// `packing` is from `LazyField::packing`.\n1721\t/// `extra` is from `LazyField::complex_extra` (must be `Some` for DRT=2/3).\n1722\t/// `n_points` is `LazyField::grid.num_data_points as usize`.\n1723\tpub fn decode_all_drt3(\n1724\t    body: &[u8],\n1725\t    packing: &PackingInfo,\n1726\t    extra: &ComplexExtra,\n1727\t    n_points: usize,\n1728\t) -> Result<Vec<f64>> {\n1729\t    match decode_drt3(body, packing, extra, n_points)? {\n1730\t        GridValues::Dense(v) => Ok(v),\n1731\t        GridValues::Masked { values, .. } => Ok(values),\n1732\t    }\n1733\t}\n1734\t\n1735\t/// Extract a single grid point from a DRT=2 or DRT=3 (complex packing) Section 7 body.\n1736\t///\n1737\t/// Decodes the full grid and returns `values[idx]`.\n1738\t///\n1739\t/// **Performance note:** For DRT=3 (spatial differencing), each call decodes\n1740\t/// the entire grid — random access is impossible because each value depends on\n1741\t/// all prior values.  When extracting multiple stations from the same field,\n1742\t/// prefer [`decode_all_drt3`] (decode once, cache, index in O(1)) to avoid\n1743\t/// paying the full-decode cost N times.\n1744\t///\n1745\t/// Returns `None` if `idx >= n_points` or if decoding fails.\n1746\t///\n1747\t/// `body` is `LazyField::section7_raw`.\n1748\t/// `packing` is `LazyField::packing`.\n1749\t/// `extra` is `LazyField::complex_extra.as_ref().unwrap()` (must be `Some` for DRT=2/3).\n1750\t/// `n_points` is `LazyField::grid.num_data_points as usize`.\n1751\tpub fn decode_point_drt3(\n1752\t    body: &[u8],\n1753\t    packing: &PackingInfo,\n1754\t    extra: &ComplexExtra,\n1755\t    n_points: usize,\n1756\t    idx: usize,\n1757\t) -> Option<f64> {\n1758\t    if idx >= n_points {\n1759\t        return None;\n1760\t    }\n1761\t    let values = decode_all_drt3(body, packing, extra, n_points).ok()?;\n1762\t    values.get(idx).copied()\n1763\t}\n1764\t\n1765\t/// Decode all fields lazily — Section 7 data is stored as raw bytes.\n1766\t///\n1767\t/// DRT=0 fields without a bitmap: `section7_raw` is populated; use\n1768\t/// [`decode_point_drt0`] for random access.\n1769\t///\n1770\t/// DRT=2/3 fields without a bitmap: `section7_raw` and `complex_extra` are\n1771\t/// both populated; use [`decode_all_drt3`] to decode the full grid once.\n1772\tpub fn decode_bytes_lazy(bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<Vec<LazyField>> {\n1773\t    let mut fields = Vec::new();\n1774\t    let mut pos = 0;\n1775\t    while pos < bytes.len() {\n1776\t        if bytes.len() - pos < 4 {\n1777\t            break;\n1778\t        }\n1779\t        if &bytes[pos..pos + 4] != b\"GRIB\" {\n1780\t            return Err(Error::BadMagic(bytes[pos..pos + 4].try_into().unwrap()));\n1781\t        }\n1782\t        let msg_len = decode_lazy_message(&bytes[pos..], &mut fields)?;\n1783\t        pos += msg_len;\n1784\t    }\n1785\t    Ok(fields)\n1786\t}\n1787\t\n1788\t// ── Lazy message builder ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n1789\t\n1790\t#[derive(Default)]\n1791\tstruct LazyFieldBuilder {\n1792\t    center: Option<u16>,\n1793\t    subcenter: Option<u16>,\n1794\t    ref_time: Option<ReferenceTime>,\n1795\t    parameter: Option<ParameterId>,\n1796\t    forecast: Option<ForecastTime>,\n1797\t    level: Option<Level>,\n1798\t    ensemble: Option<Option<Ensemble>>,\n1799\t    pdt_template: Option<u16>,\n1800\t    grid: Option<GridDefinition>,\n1801\t    gdt_template: Option<u16>,\n1802\t    packing: Option<PackingInfo>,\n1803\t    drt_template: Option<u16>,\n1804\t    complex_extra: Option<ComplexPackingExtra>,\n1805\t    has_bitmap: Option<bool>,\n1806\t    section7_raw: Option<Vec<u8>>,\n1807\t}\n1808\t\n1809\timpl LazyFieldBuilder {\n1810\t    fn build(self) -> Option<LazyField> {\n1811\t        let center = self.center?;\n1812\t        let subcenter = self.subcenter?;\n1813\t        let ref_time = self.ref_time?;\n1814\t        let parameter = self.parameter?;\n1815\t        let mut forecast =
